sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् । स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: उद्वेग न करनेवाला, सत्य, प्रिय, हितकारक भाषण तथा स्वाध्याय और अभ्यास करना  यह वाणीसम्बन्धी तप कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Speech that does not agitate, is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial, together with study and practice, is called austerity of speech.
essence: Speech becomes discipline when it tells the truth without creating harm.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Speech becomes a disciplined practice when it is calm, truthful, kind, helpful, and supported by study.
simpleInsight: Your words can become a form of self-training, not just self-expression.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still teaching Arjuna how inner discipline takes shape in daily life. After describing bodily austerity, he turns to speech: words should not disturb, should be true, pleasant, and useful, and should be backed by regular study.
modernRelevance: You are about to send a text that could inflame everything, or calm it. This verse asks whether your words reduce heat, carry truth, and actually help before you hit send.
translationLiteral: अनुद्वेगकरं (not causing agitation) / वाक्यं (speech) / सत्यं (true) / प्रियहितं (pleasant and beneficial) / च (and) / यत् (which) / स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं (study and practice) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / वाङ्मयं (of speech) / तपः (austerity) / उच्यते (is called)
